message:
fix(sync+distributed): pool waiter wakeup, resource tracker efficiency, recovery overflow

pool.rs:
- Wake a blocked acquirer when an unhealthy idle resource is rejected. Previously
  reject_unhealthy_idle_resource decremented active/total_acquisitions but never
  woke waiters, so a blocked acquire() would remain parked even though a pool
  slot became available. Now pops the first waiter and wakes it so it can retry
  with a fresh create or grab another idle resource

resource.rs:
- Replace `batch.observers = inner.observers.clone()` with
  `batch.observers.clone_from(&inner.observers)` in both
  prepare_pressure_notifications and prepare_limit_exceeded — clone_from can
  reuse the existing allocation in batch.observers instead of always allocating
  a new Vec
- Add #[allow(clippy::significant_drop_tightening)] on try_acquire and
  ResourceGuard::drop where clippy incorrectly suggests narrowing the lock
  scope but the inner reference is still borrowed by prepare_pressure_notifications
- Minor formatting normalization

recovery.rs:
- Replace arithmetic addition with saturating_add for ESI range validation in
  add_collected_with_verify. If total_source_symbols() + min_symbols overflows
  (extremely unlikely but theoretically possible with adversarial object params),
  the comparison would wrap and accept invalid ESIs. saturating_add caps at
  u32::MAX which correctly rejects nothing (safe) rather than accepting
  everything (unsafe)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
